title: Ben Horowitz - "Your ONLY job is Right Product, Right Time"
content: Ben Horowitz stresses that a founder’s sole focus should be delivering the right product at the right time. He explains how strategy emerges organically, highlighting the link between a company’s story and its plan. Horowitz advises founders on hiring, fundraising, and decision‑making amid rapid change, notes AI’s impact on teams, and stresses creativity, relationships, and generalist builders as key. He emphasizes navigating uncertainty, the reality of pivots, and that defensibility comes from solving hard problems and building strong customer bonds.

content: The author criticizes Alibaba’s Token Plan after their company purchased the team version. They tested it on OpenCode and CodeX and found it severely inferior to earlier OpenRouter trial models, causing discomfort for work use. Issues cited include stubborn behavior (using an unintended method), self‑contradiction (claiming read‑only but requesting writes), failure to follow the current workspace or skill repository, and unreliable execution that creates incorrect backups. The author documented these problems in a shared presentation.

title: 万科最值钱的资产浮出水面
content: Vanke’s 21.4 % stake in global logistics firm GLP could become its most valuable asset if GLP goes public in Hong Kong, potentially raising up to $200 bn in valuation. Vanke’s holding would then be worth $42.8‑$64.2 bn, nearly equal to its current market cap, and could provide quick, high‑quality liquidity versus its slower‑moving real‑estate portfolio. This “exit window” offers Vanke a powerful tool to ease debt and restructure assets amid ongoing financial pressures.

content: Magi is an open‑source desktop AI agent that collects and organizes users’ passive activity data—browser history, media usage, photos, calendar events, Git commits, etc.—into a five‑layer local memory system. It creates a long‑term timeline while preventing excessive storage and echo‑chamber errors. The agent uses gradual reply segments and dynamic persona shifts to feel less robotic, adjusting tone under stress. Currently in beta, Magi ships with a local MacOS client, no external data uploads, and a GitHub repository for issues and contributions.

title: 求证： Claude Code 的响应速度是不是普遍比 Cursor(opus)、Codex 慢？
content: Claude Code generally responds slower than Cursor’s Opus model or Codex. Users typically experience 1–2 minute latency for simple prompts, up to 5–10 minutes for longer tasks, with an additional 10–15 second delay before output starts. This lag is common, not limited to individual connectivity. Slower responses reduce the number of feasible calls, especially on lower‑tier plans, which may explain why some users exhaust a 100‑dollar plan quickly.

title: GPT-5.6曝光了，OpenAI砸钱宣战：换掉Claude Code
content: OpenAI disclosed that GPT‑5.6 development is underway, with internal testing already underway and an expected public release next month. The company will launch an “ultrafast” mode that doubles‑thrice existing speed, partly to counter Anthropic’s Claude Code and its new Opus 4.7 Fast mode. Anthropic offers a 50% programming quota boost, while OpenAI responds with two free months for customers switching to Codex. The convergence of rapid model iteration and widespread AI coding tools is accelerating a feedback loop toward ever faster AI capabilities.

title: 习近平与特朗普对北京峰会有何期待
content: Xi and Trump view the Beijing summit as a chance to address key bilateral issues—trade, technology, security and climate—while boosting mutual confidence. Xi seeks to reassess U.S. sanctions and trade imbalances, and to frame China’s global role positively. Trump wants to secure American economic interests and curb perceived Chinese advantages. Both leaders aim to set a constructive tone for future U.S.–China cooperation and address humanitarian concerns, especially for Uighurs.

They plan to deploy this system first for drug discovery, batteries, and fusion, targeting continuous, recursive AI advancement.

title: MiniMax 推出了 Mavis，活脱脱的 Agent「三省六部」
content: MiniMax launched “Mavis,” a multi‑agent system that uses a Leader‑Worker‑Verifier hierarchy to break long tasks into steps, check and correct outputs, and maintain isolated contexts. Mavis addresses conversational “anxiety” by pausing after each step to confirm before proceeding. It supports parallel task execution via IM platforms, shares a unified token plan, and emphasizes higher cost for higher‑quality, complex work. The system aims to improve reliability, not intelligence, by engineering strict accountability among agents.

title: 风暴中的 Manus，蝴蝶还在扇动翅膀
content: Manus, an AI agent startup, rose to prominence in early 2025 with a viral demo and rapid adoption, earning a $1 billion ARR in eight months. It introduced reusable “Skills,” a desktop agent (My Computer), mobile remote control, and a cloud‑computer service, but faced regulatory scrutiny and a 2026 government order halting a Meta buy‑out. Amidst industry shifts and compliance demands, the team has continued incremental releases, yet its once‑leading innovation has narrowed to following market trends rather than setting them.

title: 马化腾和吴泳铭，重新做公司
content: Alibaba and Tencent’s latest earnings reveal a shift from growth‑by‑expansion to operating efficiency driven by AI. Alibaba’s CEO, Wu Yongming, signals that AI income—centered on cloud, models, and chips—has entered a profitable, commercial cycle, while Tencent’s President Liu Jinping prioritizes internal AI use over external GPU rentals, tightening resource allocation. Both companies now measure success by算力 (compute) and high‑value traffic, not sheer user volume, marking a new era where AI’s cost forces tighter, smarter operations.

He admitted that Tencent’s early AI foundations were weak and that the firm has been working to catch up. Rather than racing to seize opportunities, Tencent is taking a measured path that aligns with its strengths. He criticised attempts to overtake competitors, noting past failures, and used a boat analogy to describe the lag—initially thinking the “AI ride” was solid, only to find leaks. He hopes the pace will improve.

The rocket, the tallest ever built, paused for a launch rehearsal on May 11 and is slated to fly for the first time by the end of the week. title: Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them)
content: The article traces the longevity of roguelike games, noting their origins in early terminal titles and collaborative development over Usenet and the internet. It highlights ten open‑source roguelikes—such as NetHack, Cataclysm, Dungeon Crawl, Angband, Brogue, Pixel Dungeon, Shattered, DRL, KeeperRL, and HyperRogue—that thrive thanks to active communities that continually add content, fork projects, and experiment. These communities keep the games alive, evolving mechanics and graphics while preserving the core roguelike experience.
